rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before using them in expressions or declarations.
When encountering type errors in AGREE, verify that all declared constants and equations use valid scalar types (int, real, bool) and that expressions match expected types.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always ensure that named things used in expressions have valid types and are properly declared or visible in the current scope.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid resolution errors.
When writing guarantees, relate visible outputs to visible inputs and avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Place AGREE contracts in appropriate scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings and assignments.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use scalar types: int, real, bool.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Guarantee statements should not be placed in implementation scope; they belong in component type annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not reference identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AADL scope when writing AGREE contracts.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation-level annexes; use component type annexes instead.
Do not mix invalid or unresolved identifiers with valid expressions in AGREE clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Component type and implementation binding patterns
